proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_sotorasib.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_osimertinib.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 17.39% on pembrolizumab vs 16.40% off; absolute diff +0.99 pp, chi-square p=0.0034 — pembrolizumab is associated with a small but significant marginal increase in ORR.
ORR 16.95% on sotorasib vs 16.87% off; diff +0.08 pp, chi-square p=0.83 — no marginal effect.
ORR 16.99% on olaparib vs 16.85% off; diff +0.13 pp, chi-square p=0.72 — no marginal effect.
ORR 16.55% on osimertinib vs 17.04% off; diff -0.50 pp, chi-square p=0.18 — no marginal effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among egfr_mutation-positive patients, treatment_osimertinib raises objective_response more than it does among egfr_mutation-negative patients (positive interaction on ORR).
Among kras_g12c-positive patients, treatment_sotorasib raises objective_response more than it does among kras_g12c-negative patients (positive interaction on ORR).
Among brca2_mutation-positive patients, treatment_olaparib raises objective_response more than it does among brca2_mutation-negative patients (positive interaction on ORR).

analyses.result_summary: Diff-of-diffs in ORR (osimertinib on - off) was -0.20 pp in EGFR+ vs -0.54 pp in EGFR-; logistic interaction coef +0.024, p=0.76. No matched-targeted-therapy benefit detected.
Diff-of-diffs in ORR was +0.81 pp in KRAS-G12C+ vs -0.03 pp in KRAS-G12C-; logistic interaction coef +0.059, p=0.43. Direction consistent with biology but not significant.
Diff-of-diffs in ORR was -2.16 pp in BRCA2+ vs +0.20 pp in BRCA2-; logistic interaction coef -0.18, p=0.27. No olaparib enrichment effect — direction is opposite to expected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher objective_response (positive marginal effect).
The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is larger in patients with higher pdl1_tps (positive PD-L1 x pembrolizumab interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Continuous PDL1 has a positive log-odds slope on ORR of +0.166 per unit (i.e. across 0->1 TPS), p=0.0032. Higher PD-L1 is associated with higher ORR.

Strong positive PDL1 x pembrolizumab interaction (log-odds +0.591, p=1.6e-7). ORR by PDL1-quartile in pembro vs no-pembro: Q1 16.8/16.6%, Q2 16.2/16.8%, Q3 16.3/16.7%, Q4 (highest) 20.1% vs 15.4% — pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in the top quartile of PD-L1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stk11_mutation-positive patients derive less benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response than stk11_mutation-negative patients (negative STK11 x pembrolizumab interaction; immunotherapy resistance).
keap1_mutation-positive patients derive less benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response than keap1_mutation-negative patients (negative KEAP1 x pembrolizumab interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Negative STK11 x pembrolizumab interaction (log-odds -0.211, p=0.0018). Pembrolizumab raised ORR from 16.2% to 17.7% in STK11-WT but lowered it from 17.3% to 15.8% in STK11-mut — supports immunotherapy resistance.
KEAP1 x pembrolizumab interaction is negative but small (-0.058, p=0.36); not significant. ORR change with pembro is similar in KEAP1-mut vs WT.

proposed_hypotheses.text: tmb_high-positive patients have higher objective_response than tmb_high-negative patients overall.
tmb_high-positive patients derive more benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response than tmb_high-negative patients (positive TMB x pembrolizumab interaction).

analyses.result_summary: tmb_high main-effect log-odds +0.127 (p=9.8e-7). ORR is meaningfully higher in TMB-high patients overall.
Positive TMB-high x pembrolizumab interaction (log-odds +0.184, p=0.00041). Pembro raises ORR from 16.7% to 19.7% in TMB-high vs 16.3% to 16.5% in TMB-low.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (negative prognostic effect).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response (positive prognostic effect).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Strong negative gradient: each unit of ECOG drops log-odds by 0.375 (p=1.7e-93). The single largest prognostic factor in the dataset.
Per-g/dL log-odds +0.091 (p=0.00014). Higher albumin associated with higher ORR.
log-odds +0.0001 per U/L (p=0.51). No detectable LDH effect.
log-odds -0.005 per unit NLR (p=0.38). No marginal NLR effect.
log-odds -0.0049 per mg/L (p=0.00069). Higher CRP associated with lower ORR, consistent with inflammation-as-poor-prognosis.
log-odds -0.039 per percent body weight lost (p=1.8e-32). Cachexia is a strong negative prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different objective_response rate than male patients.
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher bmi is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher education_years is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Female log-odds +0.083 (p=0.00057); ORR is modestly higher in women than men.
log-odds +0.0005 per year (p=0.67). No age effect on ORR.
log-odds +0.002 per BMI unit (p=0.35). No BMI effect.
log-odds -0.00015 per pack-year (p=0.76). No effect.
log-odds -0.003 per year of education (p=0.40). No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Squamous histology is associated with a different objective_response than adenocarcinoma.
Smoking status (current/former vs never) is associated with objective_response.
stage_iv disease is associated with lower objective_response than non-stage IV.
has_brain_mets is associated with lower objective_response.
liver_mets is associated with lower objective_response.
bone_mets is associated with different objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Squamous vs adeno log-odds +0.047 (p=0.076). Trend toward higher ORR in squamous, not significant.
vs current smokers (ref), former -0.027 (p=0.32) and never -0.061 (p=0.11). No significant differences.
stage_iv log-odds -0.295 (p=1.3e-33). Strong negative association — stage IV cuts ORR substantially.
has_brain_mets log-odds -0.252 (p=2.0e-18). Brain mets strongly negative for response.
log-odds +0.032 (p=0.33). No detectable effect.
log-odds +0.040 (p=0.18). No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ORR (objective_response) differs by race_ethnicity category, with white patients having lower response than Asian patients.
ORR differs by insurance_type, with privately insured patients having higher response than Medicaid-insured patients.
rural_residence is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Reference Asian (ORR 17.7%); white log-odds -0.083 (p=0.099) — trend toward lower ORR. Black (+0.002, p=0.97), Hispanic (-0.040, p=0.49), other (+0.055, p=0.56) not significantly different from Asian. No strong race signal.
Reference Medicaid (ORR 16.1%). Private +0.077 (p=0.040), Medicare +0.052 (p=0.16), uninsured +0.063 (p=0.35). Privately insured had significantly higher ORR than Medicaid (17.2% vs 16.1%).
log-odds -0.021 (p=0.49). No rural-vs-urban difference detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Coronary artery disease is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher fatigue_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
Comorbidities such as diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure are individually associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: log-odds -0.062 (p=0.049). Borderline significant inverse association with ORR.
log-odds -0.022 per grade (p=0.040). Higher fatigue mildly associated with lower ORR.

diabetes p=0.74, hypertension p=0.71, COPD p=0.37, CKD p=0.21, HF p=0.88, atrial fibrillation p=0.17, VTE p=0.61, autoimmune p=0.40, hep B p=0.79, hep C p=0.69, HIV p=0.37, prior malignancy p=0.44, depression/anxiety p=0.66, ILD p=0.26. None significant aside from CAD (h34).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each rare oncogenic alteration (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, msi_high) is associated with a different objective_response than wildtype.
alk_fusion is associated with a different objective_response than wildtype.
tp53_mutation is associated with lower objective_response.
pik3ca_mutation is associated with different objective_response.

analyses.code: Univariate logit for each rare alteration

analyses.result_summary: alk_fusion log-odds -0.041 (p=0.46). No effect.
tp53_mutation log-odds -0.004 (p=0.87). No effect.
pik3ca log-odds +0.074 (p=0.086). Trend, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: alk_fusion-positive patients derive less benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response than alk_fusion-negative patients (negative interaction; oncogene-driven NSCLC has poor immunotherapy response).

egfr_mutation-positive patients derive less benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response than egfr_mutation-negative patients (negative interaction; classical EGFR-driven NSCLC poor IO responders).

analyses.result_summary: ALK x pembro interaction +0.014 (p=0.90). No detectable differential effect.
EGFR x pembro interaction -0.035 (p=0.63). Direction consistent with biology (EGFR+ get less from pembro), but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_g12c-positive patients derive more benefit from treatment_sotorasib on objective_response than kras_g12c-negative patients (refined positive interaction test, since main effect was null; revisiting with explicit interaction logit and stratified ORRs).

analyses.code: ORR by kras_g12c × treatment_sotorasib (already from iter 2)
analyses.result_summary: KRAS-G12C+ on sotorasib 17.5% vs off 16.6% (+0.9 pp); KRAS-G12C- on sotorasib 16.9% vs off 16.9% (~0 pp). Direction is correct (matched targeted therapy helps target-bearing patients more) but in 2,246 G12C+ on-sotorasib patients the magnitude is too small to reach significance (interaction p=0.43). The dataset does not show a strong matched-therapy benefit in this synthetic / EHR-like cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: prior_chemotherapy is associated with lower objective_response (heavily pretreated patients respond less).
More prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with lower objective_response.
Longer years_since_diagnosis is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: log-odds -0.044 (p=0.065). Trend toward lower ORR, marginally not significant.
log-odds -0.012 per line (p=0.24). No significant effect.
log-odds -0.007 per year (p=0.32). No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: prior_immunotherapy, prior_targeted_therapy, prior_radiation, and prior_surgery are each associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: Univariate logit per prior treatment exposure
analyses.result_summary: prior_radiation +0.003 (p=0.89); prior_surgery +0.023 (p=0.35); prior_immunotherapy +0.030 (p=0.38); prior_targeted_therapy +0.038 (p=0.30). None significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 23 SNPs (snp_rs1045642 ... snp_rs1050828) is associated with objective_response after correction for multiple comparisons.

analyses.code: Univariate logit for each of 23 SNPs
analyses.result_summary: Top hits: snp_rs7412 coef +0.100 p=0.018; snp_rs1050828 coef -0.099 p=0.031. The remaining 21 SNPs are p>0.25. Bonferroni threshold for 23 tests is p<0.0022; none survive. No robust pharmacogenomic signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined: snp_rs7412 interacts with treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response (testing whether the nominal SNP signal is treatment-specific).

analyses.result_summary: Pre-checked for completeness; interaction exists but with a marginal SNP main effect that does not survive Bonferroni; no further strong evidence. (Consistent with iter 16 conclusion that SNPs lack robust signal.)

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, albumin, CRP, weight loss, PD-L1, TMB-high, STK11 mutation, and pembrolizumab plus PD-L1×pembrolizumab, STK11×pembrolizumab, and TMB×pembrolizumab interactions: the three pembrolizumab interactions remain significant while their corresponding main effects largely do not.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ age_years + sex_female + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin_g_dl + crp_mg_l + weight_loss_pct_6mo + pdl1_tps + tmb_high + stk11_mutation + treatment_pembrolizumab + pdl1_tps*pembro + stk11*pembro + tmb_high*pembro)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: ECOG -0.382 (p<1e-90), stage_iv -0.302 (p<1e-30), brain mets -0.257 (p<1e-17), weight loss -0.039 (p<1e-30), albumin +0.092 (p<0.001), CRP -0.005 (p<0.001), sex_female +0.081 (p=0.001) all significant. Critically: pembrolizumab main effect -0.132 (p=0.005) negative, PDL1×pembro +0.595 (p<1e-6), STK11×pembro -0.204 (p=0.003), TMB×pembro +0.174 (p=0.001) — all three interactions retain significance and direction after adjustment. Interpretation: pembrolizumab itself has no benefit for the average patient; benefit is tightly conditioned on PD-L1 high, TMB high, and STK11 wildtype.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined: When pembrolizumab benefit is conditioned on PD-L1 dichotomized at ≥50% (clinically meaningful threshold mirroring KEYNOTE-024), the interaction is more robust than continuous PD-L1 alone.

analyses.result_summary: PDL1≥50% × pembro interaction log-odds +0.376 (p=5.0e-11). In PD-L1 ≥50% subgroup: ORR 20.9% on pembro vs 15.6% off (+5.4 pp, chi-square p=7.7e-13). In PD-L1 <50%: ORR 16.4% on vs 16.6% off (-0.2 pp, p=0.59, no benefit). Strongly supports a clinically meaningful PD-L1 high threshold.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within PD-L1 high (≥50%) patients, pembrolizumab still confers a higher ORR (refinement focused on the responsive subgroup).

analyses.code: ORR comparison: PDL1>=50% subset, pembro vs no pembro
analyses.result_summary: n=10,749 patients with PD-L1 ≥50%; pembro arm ORR 20.93% (1118/5342) vs no-pembro 15.57% (842/5407). Chi-square p=7.7e-13. Clinically large effect: a 5.4 pp absolute and ~34% relative increase in ORR.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within PD-L1 high (≥50%) patients, the pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in stk11_mutation-wildtype patients and is absent in STK11-mutant patients (three-way refinement: PD-L1×STK11×pembrolizumab).

analyses.code: ORR by stk11_mutation × treatment_pembrolizumab within PDL1>=50%
analyses.result_summary: In PD-L1 ≥50% & STK11-WT (n=9,149): pembro ORR 21.73% vs no-pembro 15.36% (+6.4 pp). In PD-L1 ≥50% & STK11-mut (n=1,600): pembro 16.33% vs no-pembro 16.79% (-0.5 pp). The pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1 high is concentrated in STK11-wildtype; STK11-mutation eliminates it. Reinforces immunotherapy resistance role of STK11.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ECOG_ps × treatment interaction terms are non-zero — i.e., treatment effect on objective_response varies with performance status.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ ecog_ps + treatment + ecog_ps*treatment) for each treatment
analyses.result_summary: ECOG×pembrolizumab -0.036 (p=0.32); ECOG×sotorasib +0.020 (p=0.60); ECOG×olaparib -0.028 (p=0.48); ECOG×osimertinib +0.041 (p=0.31). None significant — treatment effects appear roughly constant across ECOG.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous lab values interact with treatment_pembrolizumab: in particular albumin_g_dl, crp_mg_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, nlr, ldh_u_l each modify the pembrolizumab effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ lab + pembro + lab*pembro) for each lab
analyses.result_summary: albumin×pembro -0.008 (p=0.86); crp×pembro +0.004 (p=0.17); weight-loss×pembro +0.004 (p=0.51); nlr×pembro +0.033 (p=0.0046); ldh×pembro +0.00006 (p=0.83). NLR×pembro is the only nominal hit: each 1-unit increase in NLR adds +0.033 to the pembro log-odds — i.e., higher NLR patients gain more from pembro, possibly reflecting the known association of inflammation/myeloid biology with checkpoint response. (Single-test p; not corrected.)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined synthesis: the dominant predictors of objective_response in this cohort are (1) prognostic disease/host factors (ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, weight loss, albumin, CRP); (2) sex (female>male); (3) the PD-L1 / TMB / STK11 axis interacting with pembrolizumab; while age, BMI, smoking pack-years, education, race/ethnicity (after adjustment), specific oncogenic alterations beyond TMB/STK11, and the panel of 23 SNPs do not have robust associations.

analyses.result_summary: Confirmed: ECOG (p<1e-90), stage_iv (p<1e-30), brain_mets (p<1e-17), weight loss (p<1e-30), albumin (p<0.001), CRP (p<0.001) all independently predict ORR; sex_female adds +0.08 log-odds (p=0.001); pembrolizumab is conditionally beneficial (PDL1 high, TMB high, STK11 WT); other treatments have no detectable marginal effect on ORR; SNP screen and most rare alterations are null. The signal is concentrated in a small set of variables, consistent with literature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined: After full multivariable adjustment, the association of insurance_type (Medicaid baseline) with objective_response observed in iter 9 is driven by confounding (e.g., differences in ECOG, stage IV, weight loss across insurance groups) rather than insurance per se.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ C(insurance_type) + age + sex + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin + crp + weight_loss + pdl1_tps + tmb_high + stk11 + pembro + interactions)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted insurance coefficients (vs Medicaid baseline): private +0.089 (p=0.019), Medicare +0.063 (p=0.095), uninsured +0.082 (p=0.23). Hypothesis REFUTED: the private-vs-Medicaid gap PERSISTS after adjusting for ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, weight loss, albumin, CRP, sex, age, PD-L1, TMB, STK11, and pembrolizumab effects. The coefficient is essentially unchanged (or slightly larger) compared to unadjusted (+0.077). This suggests a possible independent insurance-related disparity in response that is not explained by the prognostic and biomarker variables in the dataset, rather than mere confounding.
